mission.title: Install and Configure Cortex on Kubernetes
mission.description: Cortex is a horizontally scalable, highly available, multi-tenant long-term storage solution for Prometheus and OpenTelemetry Metrics. Installing Cortex allows you to efficiently manage and store your metrics data across multiple sources in a single cluster.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Cortex Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add cortex https://cortexproject.github.io/cortex-helm-chart
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Cortex using Helm
steps.description:
Install Cortex in a new namespace called 'cortex'.
```bash
helm install cortex cortex/cortex --namespace cortex --create-namespace --version 3.2.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that all Cortex pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace cortex
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the Cortex deployment to ensure it runs efficiently.
```bash
kubectl patch deployment cortex -n cortex -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"cortex","resources":{"limits":{"cpu":"500m","memory":"1Gi"}}}]}}}}' 
```

steps.title: Set up port forwarding for access
steps.description:
Forward a local port to the Cortex service to access it.
```bash
kubectl port-forward service/cortex -n cortex 8080:80
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Cortex Helm release from the cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall cortex --namespace cortex
```

uninstall.title: Clean up the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the Cortex namespace to remove all associated resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace cortex
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Export the current configuration and data for Cortex before upgrading.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace cortex -o yaml > cortex-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Make sure the Helm repository is up to date.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cortex
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the Cortex installation to the latest version.
```bash
helm upgrade cortex cortex/cortex --namespace cortex --version 3.2.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that all Cortex pods are running successfully after the upgrade.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace cortex
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
This issue may occur if there are misconfigurations in the Cortex deployment. Check the logs of the pods for more information.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace cortex
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If the Cortex service is not reachable, ensure that the service is running and the port forwarding is correctly set up.
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace cortex
```

troubleshooting.title: High memory usage
troubleshooting.description:
If Cortex is consuming too much memory, consider adjusting the resource limits set during installation. You can edit the deployment to change the limits.
```bash
kubectl edit deployment cortex -n cortex
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Cortex will have all pods running without errors, and you will be able to access the service via the forwarded port. Resource limits should be set appropriately to ensure optimal performance.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add cortex https://cortexproject.github.io/cortex-helm-chart
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have Helm and kubectl installed and configured to interact with your Kubernetes cluster.
